answers.answer: Bryan Mendez, Marcus Reyes, Sarah Chen, and David Kim attended the discovery call.
requiredSnippets: Bryan Mendez — CTRL.ALT.I — Solution Lead
Marcus Reyes — MCR Consulting — Partner
Sarah Chen — Pinnacle Logistics — Director of Operations
David Kim — Pinnacle Logistics — IT Manager

answers.answer: Pinnacle Logistics is processing about 800 vendor invoices per month.
requiredSnippets: Monthly invoice volume: 800 vendor invoices per month

answers.answer: The integration uses the QuickBooks Online Bills API.
requiredSnippets: Integration API: QuickBooks Online Bills API

answers.answer: Pinnacle's fiscal year close is June 30.
requiredSnippets: Fiscal year close: June 30

answers.answer: The solution is being built on N8N.
requiredSnippets: Orchestration layer: N8N

answers.answer: Patricia Walsh, Pinnacle's CFO, joined starting in the second meeting.
requiredSnippets: Patricia Walsh — Pinnacle Logistics — CFO

answers.answer: The initial ballpark was $18,000 to $22,000 with a $1,500 monthly retainer, and the final proposal was $24,500 with a $1,800 monthly retainer.
requiredSnippets: Build price: $18,000 to $22,000 (ballpark)
Monthly retainer: $1,500
Current build price: $24,500
Current monthly retainer: $1,800

answers.answer: GoHighLevel was discussed and then explicitly removed from the engagement.
requiredSnippets: GoHighLevel was deferred out of the AP automation engagement and moved to a later Q3 track

answers.answer: The work started as a six-week estimate and became an eight-week phased delivery plan.
requiredSnippets: Initial delivery estimate: six weeks from kickoff to production, assuming sandbox QuickBooks access in week one
Timeline evolved from a six-week estimate to an eight-week phased delivery plan

answers.answer: The scope started with AP automation, then added Slack approvals, then added an admin dashboard, while GoHighLevel and expense automation were deferred.
requiredSnippets: Slack approvals were added after Bryan Mendez identified manual approvals as the remaining bottleneck
An admin dashboard was added so Sarah Chen can monitor workflow activity without relying on David Kim
GoHighLevel was deferred out of the AP automation engagement and moved to a later Q3 track
Expense report automation was parked until after the AP workflow reaches production

answers.answer: The price increased because Slack approvals and then the admin dashboard were added to scope.
requiredSnippets: Slack approvals were added after Bryan Mendez identified manual approvals as the remaining bottleneck
An admin dashboard was added so Sarah Chen can monitor workflow activity without relying on David Kim
Commercials progressed from $18,000 to $22,000 plus $1,500/month to $24,500 plus $1,800/month

answers.answer: Meeting one included Marcus and David, meeting two introduced Patricia, and meeting three brought David back for the final walkthrough.
requiredSnippets: Marcus Reyes — MCR Consulting — Partner
Patricia Walsh — Pinnacle Logistics — CFO
David Kim — Pinnacle Logistics — IT Manager

answers.answer: Patricia Walsh's request to see something working in the first three weeks drove the phased proposal structure.
requiredSnippets: Structure delivery in three phases so the client sees ingestion working in the first three weeks.

answers.answer: Approval workflow was not in the initial scope, then it was added in meeting two, and by meeting three it was a core Phase 2 deliverable.
requiredSnippets: Add a Slack approval workflow to remove the post-ingestion approval bottleneck.
Phase 2: Phase 2 covers Slack approvals and QuickBooks integration

answers.answer: Slack approval workflow was added based on Bryan Mendez's recommendation rather than an initial client request.
requiredSnippets: Slack approvals were added after Bryan Mendez identified manual approvals as the remaining bottleneck

answers.answer: The engagement shows expansion signals because GoHighLevel and expense automation were deferred rather than rejected, the architecture avoids lock-in, and the stakeholders approved moving forward.
requiredSnippets: GoHighLevel was deferred out of the AP automation engagement and moved to a later Q3 track
Expense report automation was parked until after the AP workflow reaches production
Proceed with N8N as the orchestration layer as long as the client retains ownership and avoids lock-in.
Patricia Walsh and Sarah Chen approved moving forward with the proposal.

answers.answer: Sarah Chen is the operational driver, Patricia Walsh is the financial approver, and David Kim is the technical reviewer.
requiredSnippets: Sarah Chen — Pinnacle Logistics — Director of Operations — driver
Patricia Walsh — Pinnacle Logistics — CFO — approver
David Kim — Pinnacle Logistics — IT Manager — reviewer

answers.answer: Bryan moved from technical discovery and capability framing, to ROI/risk and phased planning, to proposal execution and signature next steps.
requiredSnippets: Build price: $18,000 to $22,000 (ballpark)
Risk: Extraction errors could send incorrect invoice data into QuickBooks.
Send the SOW for signature.
